chunks.page_summary: This page provides an overview of the Rheonics DTCM-PL thermal control module and the HPHT DVM sensor for simultaneous high-pressure and high-temperature density, viscosity, and temperature measurements.
product_names: Rheonics DVM HPHT ultra-high accuracy all-in-one density viscosity temperature meter
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

DTCM-PL - HPHT DVM (simultaneous density, viscosity, temperature sensor) thermal control module

Modified on: Wed, 3 Dec, 2025 at 6:39 PM

What products are involved?
Rheonics DVM HPHT ultra-high accuracy all-in-one density viscosity temperature meter (https://rheonics.com/products/hpht-pvt-density-meter-viscometer-dvm/)

What is the purpose of this article?
To give an overview of the DTCM, thermal control module for Rheonics DVM.

1. Overview
The DTCM-PL (https://rheonics.com/product-accessories/dtcm-dvm-thermal-control-module/) is Rheonics Thermal Control Module for the HPHT (High pressure and temperature) density and viscosity module DVM (https://rheonics.com/products/hpht-pvt-density-meter-viscometer-dvm/). The DTCM is custom-designed to ensure uniform temperature distribution across the DVM (Density Viscosity module), heating and cooling are achieved through a high-efficiency, fast thermal device (DTCM-PL) with an advanced model-based controller.
The DTCM-PL was designed after extensive simulation and testing to ensure high stability and accurate control of temperature around the DVM. This ensures highly accurate viscosity and density readings from the sensor.

Rheonics DVM
The DVM is an in-line sensor capable of measuring Viscosity, Density, and Temperature simultaneously. This sensor is designed to work in high-pressure (up to 30,000 psi, 2100 bar) and temperature (up to 200°C, 400°F) applications with ultra-high accuracy. This sensor requires less than 0.7 cc fluid sample to measure and its wetted parts are made of Titanium Gr. 5 (3.7165).

Figure 1. Rheonics DVM
key_facts: The DTCM-PL is Rheonics Thermal Control Module for the HPHT (High pressure and temperature) density and viscosity module DVM.
The DTCM is custom-designed to ensure uniform temperature distribution across the DVM.
Heating and cooling are achieved through a high-efficiency, fast thermal device (DTCM-PL) with an advanced model-based controller.
The DTCM-PL ensures high stability and accurate control of temperature around the DVM, leading to highly accurate viscosity and density readings.
The DVM is an in-line sensor capable of measuring Viscosity, Density, and Temperature simultaneously.
The DVM sensor is designed to work in high-pressure and high-temperature applications with ultra-high accuracy.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides detailed technical specifications, dimensions, and key features of the Rheonics DTCM-PL thermal chamber and its controller.
chunks.extracted_text:
2. Specifications of the DTCM-PL

The DTCM-PL is composed of the Thermal Chamber for the DVM sensor and a control unit. They communicate through a single cable as shown in the next example image, using an E4:SME-BOX.

Figure 2. Rheonics DTCM

Figure 3. Rheonics DTCM Dimensions

key_facts: The DTCM-PL system comprises a Thermal Chamber for a DVM sensor and a control unit that communicate via a single cable, utilizing an E4:SME-BOX.
The DTCM-PL unit has dimensions comparable to a typical desktop coffee machine.
The DTCM-PL controller integrates a 10.6-inch Human Machine Interface (HMI).
The total power consumption of the DTCM-PL, including the DVM sensor, is a maximum of 700 W.
The temperature accuracy of the DTCM-PL is equivalent to that of a DVM (Class AA Pt1000).
A drawing for the DTCM-G2 is available for download at the provided URL.
warnings_notes: For applications requiring higher temperatures than the DTCM-PL's range, the DTCM-BH model should be used.

chunks.page_summary: This page explains the advantages of the Rheonics DTCM for temperature control in viscosity and density measurements, compares it to alternative systems, and provides additional resources.
chunks.extracted_text:
3. Why use the DTCM?
DTCM eliminates the requirement of bulky and expensive thermal test chambers (also known as climate chambers) for temperature control. It reduces the size of the equipment to that of a small coffee machine like Nespresso while achieving 10 times better stability and uniformity as compared to a typical 100-liter commercial thermal chamber. It enables HPHT (High-Pressure High-Temperature) fluid analysis with DVM (Density Viscosity Module) in the field and mobile units without compromising data integrity.

Relevance of Temperature in Viscosity and Density readings
When measuring the viscosity and density of a fluid, the temperature should be controlled, since a slight change in temperature can have an impact on the viscosity and density of a fluid.
The DTCM is a solution for Rheonics DVM, which allows the user to control the temperature of the fluid, maintaining correct temperature distribution around the DVM, while measuring the viscosity and density of the fluid.
Temperature accuracy is also key when performing calibration verifications.

4. Alternatives Temperature Control Systems
When temperature control is required for the DVM operation, some alternatives to the DTCM are baths and thermal ovens. These are commonly used but have disadvantages predominantly in the form factor and temperature accuracy.

key_facts: The DTCM eliminates bulky and expensive thermal test chambers, reducing equipment size.
The DTCM achieves 10 times better stability and uniformity compared to a typical 100-liter commercial thermal chamber.
The DTCM enables HPHT (High-Pressure High-Temperature) fluid analysis with DVM in the field and mobile units.
Temperature control is crucial for accurate viscosity and density readings, as slight temperature changes can impact these properties.
The DTCM is a solution for Rheonics DVM to precisely control fluid temperature and maintain correct temperature distribution.
Temperature accuracy is essential for calibration verifications.
Alternatives to DTCM (baths, thermal ovens) have disadvantages in form factor and temperature accuracy.
